0.q: The ArKS gene cloned from A. roxburghii improved the salt tolerance of plants under salt stress by regulating GA content... the expression of ArKS genes and the GAs accumulation was downregulated under the salt stress.

1.q: In plants, trehalose synthesis is first catalyzed by trehalose-6-phosphate synthase (TPS), which transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to glucose-6-phosphate (G6P) to produce trehalose-6-phosphate (T6P).

2.q: KS plays a crucial role in the biosynthesis of GAs in plants. The ArKS gene cloned from A. roxburghii improved the salt tolerance of plants under salt stress by regulating GA content.

3.q: The accumulation of T6P was 1.22 and 1.50 times of that after 4 d and 16 d by NaCl stress, indicating that ArTPS may be involved in increasing the T6P content in salt stress responses.
